summary: Draft tracking PR for the mnname menu unit. The local dump has no changed files or diff because the PR was closed unmerged with zero current commits, but the decomp.dev report records measurable matching progress in main/melee/mn/mnname: +3184 matched bytes overall, 10 newly matched items, and 13 improved unmatched items.
what_changed: PR body only says "Tracking PR as per usual"; no implementation diff or changed file list is available in the slice.
The decomp.dev GALE01 report recorded matched code rising to 59.80%, a +0.08% project-wide increase and +3184 matched bytes.

10 mnname items were reported as new 100% matches: mnName_80237D94, fn_8023A0BC, mnName_80239878, DeleteName, mnName_80238754, mnName_80238AE0, mnName_8023749C, IsNameListFull, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_80238A04.

13 mnname items/sections were reported as improved but not fully matched, including mnName_80238C34, mnName_80239A24, mnName_8023A59C, mnName_8023AC40, mnName_MainInput, fn_80239574, mnName_8023A290, mnName_SortNames, mnName_8023A9B4, mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput, CompareNameStrings, .bss, and .sdata2.
The PR was closed without merge; pr.json reports merged=false, merged_at=null, commits=0, additions=0, deletions=0, and changed_files=0 at dump time.
smart_moves: Using decomp.dev progress reporting on a tracking PR preserved useful per-symbol match data even though the final PR diff is empty.
Work was concentrated in a single menu unit, main/melee/mn/mnname, making the matching progress easy to audit by function/item.
The report separates full new matches from partial improvements, which helps future contributors decide which mnname functions still need matching work.
decomp_lessons: For tracking PRs that may be reset or closed unmerged, the bot report can be the only remaining evidence of matching progress; preserve symbol lists and percentages.
Small byte deltas can finish nearly matched functions: mnName_8023749C, IsNameListFull, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_80238A04 each reached 100% with only +2 to +4 reported bytes.
Partial high-percent matches such as fn_80239574 at 99.92%, mnName_8023A290 at 97.24%, and mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput at 96.24% are good follow-up targets.
Because no diff is present, do not infer concrete struct layouts, control-flow rewrites, or matching tricks from this PR alone.
naming_conventions: The unit uses the mnName_ prefix for many address-based placeholder names, e.g. mnName_80237D94 and mnName_80238C34.

Some functions already have descriptive PascalCase-style names without the mnName_ prefix, including DeleteName, IsNameListFull, CompareNameStrings, mnName_SortNames, mnName_MainInput, and mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput.
The report preserves fn_ placeholders for still-unknown functions such as fn_8023A0BC and fn_80239574; no review evidence explains better names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly snippets or source diff are available in the slice, so specific tactics cannot be verified.
The decomp.dev report indicates practical progress from many per-function matches and near-matches within one unit rather than broad cross-system edits.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The only issue comment is the automated decomp.dev GALE01 matching report.
The PR was a draft and closed unmerged; no reviewer rationale for closure is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching main/melee/mn/mnname after pr-2348 to see whether the partial matches were completed.
Search for mnName_80238C34 and fn_80239574 in subsequent history; they were high-impact or near-complete unmatched items in this report.
Look for a successor to branch jj/mnname or another jurrejelle mnname PR because this tracking PR closed without merge.
Check current source for named functions DeleteName, IsNameListFull, CompareNameStrings, mnName_SortNames, mnName_MainInput, and mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput.
